criteria.description: Phase 2.7 (Exemplar anchor selection) executed before Phase 3 (PRODUCT.md authoring). Visible in the agent's reasoning trace OR in direction.md provenance.
Target brand_axes computed from the resolved direction + extracted current state. Tags include at least 'editorial' and 'niche' (plus 'tactile' or 'civic' if derived). Open tags, not a fixed enum.
Both plugin-side (plugins/stardust/exemplars/) and project-side (stardust/exemplars/) were checked. Project-side empty case handled without error.

All four qualifying entries (editorial-civic-2024, tactile-publishing-2025, archival-museum-2023, slop-fintech-stock-2024) appear in the qualifying set. Filter rule applied: ≥2 tag overlap OR 1 tag overlap with verdict stunning.

Both non-qualifying entries (corporate-saas-2025, playful-d2c-2024) are excluded. They never appear in direction.md or in the anchor citations. No 'fill the slot' substitution from non-overlapping brand_axes.
Up to 3 stunning anchors surfaced. With 3 qualifying stunning entries, all three are cited. Order ranked by tag-overlap richness; ties broken by critique_mode (qualified > quick) then by entry age.
Exactly 1 slop anchor surfaced (slop-fintech-stock-2024). Cited in direction.md alongside stunning anchors. If no slop entry qualifies, the slot is skipped (not substituted).

stardust/direction.md contains a # Anchors section (written at Phase 5) listing each anchor with id, verdict, brand_axes, why, and moves the entry exhibits. Section present even when corpus is thin or empty (with appropriate note).
Anchors are referenced as constraints on the design space, not copied as templates. The proposed direction does not literally replicate anchor moves; the anchor citations inform but do not bind.

If stardust emits a stardust-observed candidate, it complies with all five gates: source verdict stunning or strong; structural axis only; unambiguous pattern; not matching existing §1a; cap of 2 per session. If no candidate is emitted, that is also acceptable (the gate is conservative).
If a candidate is emitted, a one-line user-visible note surfaces ('Observed a pattern in <exemplar-id> not yet named in the catalog; saved as candidate/<axis>/<name>'). No silent emission.
No candidate moves were promoted to divergence-toolkit.md §1a from runtime. The catalog file is unchanged after this run.
Anchor selection is traceable in direction.md: which corpus was read (plugin-side / project-side), which entries were filtered out, what brand_axes were used. Provenance block per artifact-map.md.
